name: Clean Tests
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 9: Unit Tests
problem: Test code treated as second-class code becomes messy and brittle, causing tests to be abandoned when production code changes, leaving the production code without a safety net for refactoring.
steps: Apply the same care to test code as to production code: name tests clearly, keep them small, eliminate duplication.
Follow the BUILD-OPERATE-CHECK pattern: arrange, act, assert with clear visual separation.
Write one logical concept (one assert cluster) per test to make failures self-diagnosing.
Create domain-specific testing utilities and assertion helpers to keep test bodies readable.
Keep tests independent: no shared mutable state between test cases.
